claude_code_plugin.description: Passive domain intelligence - Microsoft 365 / Google Workspace tenant identification, email security observation (DMARC, DKIM, SPF, MTA-STS, BIMI), SaaS fingerprinting, certificate-transparency findings, and posture analysis. Bundles the recon MCP server and a skill that teaches Claude when and how to use it.
claude_code_plugin.name: recon

guidance_files.title: recon - VS Code (with GitHub Copilot) install

frontmatter.description: Passive domain intelligence - Microsoft 365 / Google Workspace tenant identification, email security configuration (DMARC, DKIM, SPF, MTA-STS, BIMI), SaaS fingerprinting from DNS, certificate-transparency findings, related-domain discovery. Use when a domain name appears alongside phrases like "what does <company> use", "tenant", "DMARC", "email security posture", "SaaS stack", "fingerprint", "passive recon", or "vendor diligence". Strictly passive - never use for active scanning, port scans, credentialed access, or vulnerability checks.
frontmatter.name: recon

frontmatter.description: Triage fingerprint-discovery candidates from recon's CNAME-chain classifier. Reads either a single `recon <domain> --json --include-unclassified` payload or a `candidates.json` produced by `validation/triage_candidates.py`, then proposes new `cname_target` fingerprint stanzas for `surface.yaml`. Use when the user asks to "find new fingerprints", "what SaaS are we missing", "discover unclassified CNAME targets", or wants to extend recon's catalog from a fresh scan or validation corpus run.
frontmatter.name: recon-fingerprint-triage

commands.summary: Domain intelligence from the command line.
Look up a domain. This is the default command.
Look up multiple domains from a file.
Mine a single domain for fingerprint candidates in one shot.
Check connectivity to all data sources.
Check for and install the latest recon release.
Compare the current lookup against the last cached TenantInfo.
MCP server commands: start the stdio server, install client config, run a self-check.
Install the recon MCP server config into a client's config file.
End-to-end MCP self-check.
Manage the CT subdomain cache and TenantInfo result cache.
Show CT cache state for a domain, or list all cached domains.
Clear both CT subdomain cache and TenantInfo result cache.
Inspect the built-in fingerprint catalog.
List built-in fingerprints.
Search fingerprints by slug, name, category, or detection pattern.
Show the full definition of a single fingerprint.
Scaffold a new fingerprint entry, run checks, print YAML.
Run one fingerprint against a domain corpus and report which match.
Validate fingerprint YAML files and flag duplicate slugs.
Inspect the built-in signal catalog.
List every built-in signal, grouped by category.
Search signals by name, category, description, or candidate slug.
Show the full definition of a single signal.

resources.name: Fingerprint catalog
resources.summary: Full SaaS fingerprint catalog as JSON. Each entry carries slug, name, category, confidence tier, M365 flag, match_mode, provider/display group, detection count, and a compact detection summary. Use to answer 'what services can recon identify?'.

resources.name: Posture profile catalog
resources.summary: Built-in posture profile lenses as JSON. Each entry carries name, description, focus categories, category/signal boost multipliers, excluded signals, and any profile-specific note. Use to answer 'which posture lens fits this target?' before calling analyze_posture with a profile argument.

resources.name: JSON output schema
resources.summary: The recon JSON-output contract as a JSON Schema, the same document published at docs/recon-schema.json. Use it to self-describe the shape of `recon <domain> --json` output (and the batch / delta modes in its $defs) without an external fetch. The schema's own description field states the contract version and the additive-change policy.

resources.name: Signal catalog
resources.summary: Derived intelligence signals recon can emit, as JSON. Each entry carries name, category, confidence, description, min_matches, candidate slugs, contradicts/requires relationships, and the positive-when-absent inversion set. Use to answer 'what higher-order observations can recon derive from fingerprint matches?'.

resources.name: Generated surface inventory
resources.summary: Generated, non-contractual map of recon's local CLI, MCP, JSON-schema, and agent-integration surfaces. Use it to choose local commands, tools, resources, and guidance files without reading repository files or making network calls.

tools.name: analyze_posture
tools.summary: Analyze a domain's configuration posture and return neutral observations.

tools.name: assess_exposure
tools.summary: Summarize a domain's public configuration evidence for defensive review.

tools.name: chain_lookup
tools.summary: Recursively resolve a domain and its related domains.

tools.name: clear_ephemeral_fingerprints
tools.summary: Remove all ephemeral fingerprints from the current session.

tools.name: cluster_verification_tokens
tools.summary: Cluster a list of domains by shared site-verification tokens.

tools.name: compare_postures
tools.summary: Compare the public configuration evidence of two domains side by side.

tools.name: discover_fingerprint_candidates
tools.summary: Mine a single domain for new-fingerprint candidates.

tools.name: explain_dag
tools.summary: Render the v1.9 Bayesian evidence DAG for a domain.

tools.name: explain_signal
tools.summary: Query a specific signal's trigger conditions and current state for a domain.

tools.name: export_graph
tools.summary: Return the raw CT co-occurrence graph (nodes + weighted edges).

tools.name: find_hardening_gaps
tools.summary: Identify hardening opportunities in a domain's public configuration.

tools.name: get_fingerprints
tools.summary: List all loaded fingerprints with slugs, categories, and detection types.

tools.name: get_infrastructure_clusters
tools.summary: Return the CT co-occurrence community-detection report for a domain.

tools.name: get_posteriors
tools.summary: Compute v1.9 Bayesian-network posteriors over high-level claims.

tools.name: get_signals
tools.summary: List all loaded signals with rules, layers, and conditions.

tools.name: inject_ephemeral_fingerprint
tools.summary: Inject a temporary fingerprint for the current session.

tools.name: list_ephemeral_fingerprints
tools.summary: List all ephemeral fingerprints loaded in the current session.

tools.name: lookup_tenant
tools.summary: Look up domain intelligence - company name, email provider, tenant ID,

tools.name: reevaluate_domain
tools.summary: Re-evaluate a previously looked-up domain against current fingerprints.

tools.name: reload_data
tools.summary: Reload fingerprint and signal definitions from disk.

tools.name: simulate_hardening
tools.summary: Re-compute the public-evidence index with hypothetical fixes.

tools.name: test_hypothesis
tools.summary: Test a theory about a domain against signals and evidence.

private_data_policy: Contains no target-domain output, corpus lines, tenant IDs, or validation results.
purpose: Derived maintainer and agent-author map of recon's local CLI, MCP, and JSON-schema surfaces. This is a drift guard, not a runtime API contract.
